title_guess: Lower Middleton Cheney & Stowe weekend (with Thomas & Naomi)

pages.text:
__Thursday June 14__
We drove in convoy from Westfield Rd -> __Lower Middleton Cheney__. The rain tipped down + the M40 + services flooded. Thomas + Naomi went in the red car, Simon, Eve + Tonic in the van. There were huge traffic jams. We walked to the New Inn for supper.        Burgess Farm [illegible][illegible][illegible][illegible]

__Friday__
We had a superb walk, about 5 miles, cow parsley + elderflower. Sheep, cows, church, [?Tudor plaster?] we were not on a public footpath when we neared Lord Heseltine's house, (1769) there was a lime, oak, chestnut avenue. Naomi washed her [?stuff/feet] feet in Lord Heseltine's lake. The sun shone hot. We reached the shop + it got cloudy + we again went to the New Inn for lunch. The rain lashed down again. Will Thomas + Naomi be able to cook a fish bbq for supper?

Well, yes they did! Simon helped light the bbq + just as it caught the heavens opened again, as I had predicted. So they shoved it under a nearby metal plant trough until the worst of the rain was passed. Naomi made a salad + wrapped salmon + corn on the cob in tin foil. It wa[s]

delicious!
__Saturday__
We slept well & it was a bit chilly in the morning so we breakfasted in the van. Then Naomi took Thomas to Banbury Station to go back to play a concert at St. Augustines. She returned & we had coffee before setting off for __Stowe__.
We walked near the Elysian field which was very beautiful. Lots of temples & pillars, & views. It was built by Lord Cobham, 1760? Lovely big trees & places to picnic. Sold to NT by school in 1989.

We walked, had a picnic at the van & walked again & had a cup of tea. V. large fish, geese.

We were very lucky with the weather. As we drove from the carpark (Naomi to visit Keisha in Suffolk, Simon & Eve home) to the lodge, it started to rain again. It was a very good weekend, not least because Naomi taught us what kissing gates are for.
